name: Agent Governance
core_question: How does governance scale in decentralized systems without central authority?
description: Decision-making, trust, voting, conflict resolution in federated agent meshes. How do autonomous nodes agree? What happens when they don't? How do you govern what you can't control?
human_world_bridge: Every governance problem in the mesh is a governance problem humans face. Decentralized voting, trust without central authority, handling bad actors — these are the problems of democracy, international relations, open-source communities. Agent governance is a laboratory for human governance.
research_axes: Trust propagation and decay in peer networks
Protocol evolution: how do standards change when no one owns them?
